idea.raw_input: Add a feature in the budget tracker app where users can see the values of how much they have per account. Can be seen on the home page and a separate page for accounts. Follow the design template of the other pages.

overview.problem: Users viewing the Accounts page see account balances but cannot tap into an account to see its transaction history, statistics, or perform transfers between accounts.
overview.solution: Add an Account Detail screen accessible by tapping an account card, showing filtered transaction history, income/expense statistics, adjustment capability, and account-to-account transfer.
overview.target_user: Budget app users who track multiple accounts (cards, bank accounts) and need per-account visibility.
overview.success_metric: Users can view per-account transaction history, see income/expense breakdown, make adjustments, and transfer between accounts without leaving the app.

screens.name: Account Detail Screen
screens.purpose: Shows a single account's transaction history, statistics (income/expense breakdown), and provides actions for adjustments and transfers.

features.name: Account Detail View
features.description: Tapping an account card on the Accounts page navigates to a detail screen showing the account name, current balance, and a tabbed or sectioned view of transactions and statistics.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Accounts page is displayed, when the user taps an account card, then the Account Detail screen opens with the correct account name and balance.
Given the Account Detail screen is open, when the user views the transactions tab, then only transactions belonging to that account are shown.
Given the Account Detail screen is open, when the user views the statistics tab, then total income and total expense for that account are displayed.

features.name: Account Adjustment
features.description: Users can add an adjustment transaction (income or expense) directly from the Account Detail screen to correct balance discrepancies.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Account Detail screen is open, when the user taps 'Adjust', then a dialog opens to enter adjustment amount and description.
Given the adjustment dialog is submitted, when the user confirms, then a new transaction is created with the selected account ID and the balance updates.

features.name: Account Transfer
features.description: Users can transfer funds between accounts, which creates a negative transaction in the source account and a positive transaction in the destination account.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Account Detail screen is open, when the user taps 'Transfer', then a dialog opens to select destination account and enter amount.
Given the transfer dialog is submitted, when the user confirms, then a negative transaction is created in the source account and a positive transaction in the destination account.
Given only one account exists, when the user opens the transfer dialog, then no destination accounts are available and a message is shown.

out_of_scope: Editing or deleting existing transactions from the detail screen

criteria_results.criterion: Given transactions exist with different accountIds, when getBalancePerAccount is called, then each account's balance is the sum of its transaction amounts.
Given no transactions for an account, when balance is called, then that account shows 0.00.
criteria_results.reason: Map returns null for missing keys — callers use ?? 0.0 default

criteria_results.criterion: The method returns Map<String, double> where keys are account IDs.
Given an Account and balance, when rendered, then the card shows the account icon, name, and formatted balance.
Card follows existing Fortuna card design patterns (container with border, shadow, padding).
Balance is formatted with commas and 2 decimal places.
Given accounts exist, when the screen loads, then all accounts from AccountSettingsModel are displayed.
Each account row shows icon, name, and computed balance from BudgetModel.getBalancePerAccount().
The page header follows the same style as TransactionHistoryScreen header.
criteria_results.reason: Same Fortuna header pattern with circle avatar + title

criteria_results.criterion: Only custom accounts (not Cash) can be deleted via a delete icon.
criteria_results.reason: Delete button hidden for default account (cash)

criteria_results.criterion: An empty message is shown if only Cash account exists with zero balance.

prd_checks.reason: Only shows account balances per FEA-003

criteria_results.criterion: Given accounts with balances exist, when rendered, then the card shows each account name and formatted balance.
A 'View all' text button is shown at the bottom of the card.
criteria_results.reason: VIEW ALL GestureDetector at top-right of card header

criteria_results.criterion: Given 'View all' is tapped, then the app navigates to the Accounts page.
The card follows existing Fortuna card design patterns.
criteria_results.reason: Same Container decoration as other dashboard cards

criteria_results.criterion: Given the dashboard is loaded, when AccountBalancesCard is added, then it appears in the layout with proper animation.
criteria_results.reason: Added as _AnimatedCard with index 5 in both layouts

criteria_results.criterion: The mobile layout shows AccountBalancesCard after the existing cards.
The desktop layout includes AccountBalancesCard in the grid.
The number of animation controllers is updated to match the new card count.
No analyzer errors after the change.
Given the app is running, when /accounts is navigated to, then AccountsScreen is displayed.
An Accounts tab appears in the bottom navigation bar with appropriate icon.
The tab follows the same styling as other bottom nav tabs.

state_management.justification: App already uses Provider package with ChangeNotifier. No new state management needed — Account Detail screen reuses BudgetModel and AccountSettingsModel.

navigation.justification: App already uses go_router. Adding a nested route for account detail with accountId parameter.

local_storage.justification: Existing pattern — BudgetModel already serializes transactions to SharedPreferences with accountId field.

api_client.justification: App is fully local, no backend.

observability.justification: Small app, no remote observability needed.

tickets.title: Implement AccountDetailScreen
tickets.description: Main account detail screen showing account name, balance, transaction list, statistics, and action buttons for adjustment and transfer.
inputs: SCR-001: Account Detail Screen from PRD
acceptance_criteria: Screen receives accountId as GoRouter path parameter
Screen displays correct account name and balance from BudgetModel
Screen shows filtered transaction list for the selected account
Screen shows statistics: total income, total expense, transaction count
Screen has 'Adjust' button that opens AdjustmentDialog
Screen has 'Transfer' button that opens TransferDialog
Back navigation returns to Accounts page
Handles non-existent accountId gracefully (redirects back)
tickets.coder_notes: Use Consumer2<BudgetModel, AccountSettingsModel>. Follow existing Fortuna design patterns from accounts_screen.dart. Use GoRouter path '/accounts/:accountId'.

tickets.title: Implement AccountTransactionList widget
tickets.description: Reusable widget that displays a filtered list of transactions for a specific account, reusing TransactionListItem from the existing transactions feature.
acceptance_criteria: Widget accepts accountId and list of transactions
Filters transactions to show only those matching the accountId
Reuses TransactionListItem for consistent styling
Shows empty state when no transactions exist for the account
Groups transactions by date (Today, Yesterday, date header)
tickets.coder_notes: Extract the grouping logic from TransactionHistoryScreen._groupTransactions or reuse it. Show empty state with 'No transactions for this account yet'.

tickets.title: Implement AccountStatisticsCard widget
tickets.description: Widget showing income/expense statistics for a specific account: total income, total expense, net change, and transaction count.
acceptance_criteria: Widget accepts accountId and list of transactions
Calculates and displays total income (sum of positive amounts)
Calculates and displays total expenses (sum of negative amounts as absolute value)
Calculates and displays net change (income - expenses)
Shows transaction count
Uses Fortuna color tokens for income (tertiary) and expense (error) colors
tickets.coder_notes: Follow the card styling pattern from AccountBalanceCard. Use FortunaColors for consistency.

tickets.title: Implement AdjustmentDialog
tickets.description: Dialog for adding an adjustment transaction (income or expense) to the current account.
acceptance_criteria: Dialog has amount input field with validation
Dialog has description input field
Dialog has income/expense toggle
On confirm, calls BudgetModel.addTransaction with the correct accountId
On cancel, closes dialog without creating transaction
Validates amount is positive and non-zero
tickets.coder_notes: Use AlertDialog with Form. Follow the pattern from edit_budget_dialog.dart. Category should be 'Adjustment' for adjustments.

tickets.title: Implement TransferDialog
tickets.description: Dialog for transferring funds from the current account to another account.
acceptance_criteria: Dialog shows dropdown of available destination accounts (excluding current account)
Dialog has amount input field with validation
On confirm, creates negative transaction in source account and positive transaction in destination account
On cancel, closes dialog without creating transactions
Shows message when no other accounts exist
Validates amount is positive and non-zero
tickets.coder_notes: Use DropdownButtonFormField for destination account selection. Create two transactions: one negative (source) and one positive (destination) with description 'Transfer to/from [account name]'.

tickets.title: Update AccountsScreen to navigate to detail on tap
tickets.description: Modify AccountBalanceCard in AccountsScreen to be tappable and navigate to AccountDetailScreen.
acceptance_criteria: Tapping an account card navigates to /accounts/{accountId}
Uses context.push() for navigation
Visual feedback on tap (InkWell or similar)
tickets.coder_notes: Wrap AccountBalanceCard in GestureDetector or InkWell. Use context.push('/accounts/${account.id}').

tickets.title: Add account detail route to app_router.dart
tickets.description: Add GoRoute for /accounts/:accountId pointing to AccountDetailScreen.
acceptance_criteria: Route /accounts/:accountId is registered in GoRouter
Route passes accountId path parameter to AccountDetailScreen
Route is outside ShellRoute (no bottom nav on detail screen)
tickets.coder_notes: Add as a separate GoRoute at the same level as the ShellRoute (like AddTransactionScreen). Use GoRoute(path: '/accounts/:accountId', ...).
